title: Nursery Rhyme. CCII. Riddles.
content:
[A needle and thread.]

        Old mother Twitchett had but one eye,
        And a long tail which she let fly;
        And every time she went over a gap,
        She left a bit of her tail in a trap.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCII. Riddles." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
